issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule removes the default focus outline (outline: none) without providing any visible replacement such as a box‑shadow, colored border, or non‑zero outline. Keyboard‑only users lose the visual focus indicator, making navigation impossible for many.
issuesFound.fix: Line 322: :focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); /* add visible focus style, e.g.: box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); */ }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)

issuesFound.fix: Line 327: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; /* add visible focus style, e.g.: box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); */ }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from screen readers (.sr-only)
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which does not hide the element from assistive technologies and also fails to hide it visually. This defeats its purpose of providing screen‑reader‑only text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 618: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0 0 0 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from screen readers (.not-sr-only)
issuesFound.explanation: The .not-sr-only class mirrors the incorrect .sr-only implementation, claiming to hide content from AT while actually leaving it visible and accessible, causing confusion and potential duplicate content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 639: .not-sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0 0 0 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn (transform on :active)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn component defines a transition for transform and changes its transform on :active (translateY(1px)). No @media (prefers-reduced-motion) rule disables or overrides this motion, causing unwanted animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover rule animates the button with a transform (translateY(-1px)) and includes a transition for transform, but there is no prefers‑reduced‑motion media query that disables this movement, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who reduce motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 720: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg); transform: translateY(-1px); text-decoration: none; }
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes the outline on focus without any replacement, so keyboard users cannot see when the table region is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1000: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm (min-height: 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20 px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum 44 px target size, making it hard to tap for finger users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: .btn-sm { min-height: 20px; /* increase to at least 44px */ min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon (min-width/min-height: 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only buttons are only 20 px × 20 px, below the required 44 px target area for touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 630: .btn-icon { min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px; /* increase to at least 44px */ min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on input[type="checkbox"] (20 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Custom checkboxes are rendered at 20 px × 20 px, which is smaller than the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum 44 px target size, hindering finger users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: input[type="checkbox"] { width: 20px; height: 20px; /* increase to at least 44px */ width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body sets word-spacing: -0.2em; which compresses spacing between words, reducing legibility for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 224: word-spacing: -0.2em; /* change to a non‑negative value, e.g.: */ word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed – colour alone distinguishes links
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes underlines ( text‑decoration: none ) without providing another visual cue such as a distinct colour, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 253: a { text-decoration: none; color: var(--colour-brand-primary); /* ensure link colour differs from body text */ }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: .btn has a transition that animates transform, causing motion that does not respect users who prefer reduced motion. No @media (prefers‑reduced‑motion) override is provided.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button in the hero section transitions transform, creating motion that is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 650: .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); }  
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image animates transform on hover, but no reduced‑motion media query disables this animation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 700: .hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); }  
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .feature-card (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards animate transform on hover without a reduced‑motion fallback, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 750: .feature-card { transition: border-color var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); }  
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards animate transform on hover, lacking a reduced‑motion override.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 800: .pricing-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); }  
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card--featured (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card animates transform (on hover) without a reduced‑motion safeguard.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 820: .pricing-card--featured { transition: /* existing */; }  
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .stat-item (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: Stat items animate transform on hover, but no reduced‑motion media query disables it.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 840: .stat-item { transition: background-color var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); }  
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .product-card (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards animate transform on hover without a reduced‑motion override.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 860: .product-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); }  
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer remains in accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close()` function hides the drawer by removing CSS classes and toggling scroll‑lock, but it never sets `aria-hidden="true"` (or the `hidden` attribute) on the drawer element. Consequently, the off‑canvas navigation remains focusable and readable by screen readers even though it is visually hidden, violating WCAG 1.3.2 and 4.1.2.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer remains in accessibility tree when closed

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (`.nav-toggle`) sets aria-expanded but only adds aria-controls if the drawer element has an id. The drawer (`#nav-menu`/`#main-nav`) lacks an id, so the button has no aria-controls, breaking the relationship between the trigger and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Add an id to the drawer element (e.g., id="mobile-nav-drawer") and ensure the toggle button has aria-controls="mobile-nav-drawer".

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu.register the trigger button receives aria-haspopup and aria-expanded, but aria-controls is only set if the panel has an id. Many dropdown panels lack an id, leaving the trigger without aria-controls, which prevents assistive technologies from linking the button to its menu.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each dropdown panel has a unique id (e.g., id="nav‑services‑panel") and that the corresponding trigger button includes aria-controls="nav‑services‑panel".

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-controls attribute when id is absent
issuesFound.explanation: faqRegistry.parse creates aria-controls on the trigger only if the panel has an id. If the FAQ item lacks an id or the panel does not have one, the trigger ends up without aria-controls, breaking the disclosure relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Generate a unique id for each panel (e.g., id="faq‑panel‑1") and set the trigger’s aria-controls to that id when missing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel missing role="region" when aria-labelledby is set but role is omitted
issuesFound.explanation: The panel receives aria-labelledby but may not have role="region", which is recommended for disclosure widgets to convey the panel’s purpose to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each FAQ panel element includes role="region" in addition to aria-labelledby.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update (search suggestions) not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._debouncedFetch updates the suggestion list but does not write to the dedicated live‑region #search-status until after the fetch completes. While the status element exists, the suggestion list itself is not announced, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of new suggestions.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering the suggestion list, set searchStatus.textContent to a brief announcement (e.g., "5 suggestions available") or use liveRegion.announce to announce the number of results.

issuesFound.title: Product filter “show/hide” animation does not set aria-hidden on hidden cards
issuesFound.explanation: filterAnimation.run removes cards from the DOM and sets hidden and aria-hidden on hidden elements, but when cards are merely hidden via CSS (e.g., opacity 0) without hidden attribute, aria-hidden may not be applied, causing hidden cards to remain in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure every card that is hidden by the animation has setAttr(card, 'aria-hidden', 'true') and card.setAttribute('hidden','') immediately before the fade‑out.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria-controls for the price display region
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle (`[data-billing-toggle]`) updates aria-pressed but does not reference the region that displays the price, so screen readers cannot associate the toggle with the price change.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-controls="price-display" to the toggle button, where price-display is the id of the element showing the current price.

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan cards missing aria-label indicating recommended status
issuesFound.explanation: When a plan is highlighted as featured, the code adds a CSS class but does not add an aria-label or aria-describedby to convey “Recommended” to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Append aria-label="Recommended plan" or a visually hidden element describing the recommendation to the highlighted plan card.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “Open all” button does not have aria-controls or aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The “Open all” control triggers faqAccordion.openAll but lacks aria-controls or aria-expanded attributes, so its state is not conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-controls="faq-list" and toggle aria-expanded between true/false when the button is activated.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack aria-controls and aria-live announcements for slide changes
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel’s previous/next buttons update the visible slide but do not update aria-controls to point to the active slide nor announce the slide change via a live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-controls="slide‑{index}" to each button and call liveRegion.announce with the new slide number after each navigation.

issuesFound.title: File upload zone does not set aria-describedby to describe accepted file types and size limits
issuesFound.explanation: The drag‑and‑drop upload area provides no accessible description of allowed file types or size limits, making it difficult for screen‑reader users to know what files are acceptable.
issuesFound.fix: Add an off‑screen description element (e.g., Accepts JPEG, PNG, PDF up to 10 MB) and reference it with aria-describedby="upload‑desc" on the upload zone.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer not marked with `aria‑hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is shown/hidden by adding/removing CSS classes, but when it is hidden no `aria-hidden="true"` is applied, so the panel remains exposed to screen readers even though it is visually invisible.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav overlay missing `aria-hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay element (`_overlay`) is hidden via the `hidden` attribute but never receives `aria-hidden`, causing it to stay in the accessibility tree while invisible.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu panels lack `aria‑hidden` when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: Panels are hidden by adding a CSS class and later setting `hidden`, but no `aria-hidden` attribute is toggled, leaving hidden menus perceivable by AT.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels missing `aria‑hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ answer panels are hidden with the `hidden` attribute but never receive `aria-hidden="true"`. Screen readers may still read the content when the panel is collapsed.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing explicit `role="button"` when not a `<button>` element
issuesFound.explanation: Some FAQ items use `<div>` or `<span>` as the clickable trigger. Without `role="button"` assistive tech does not convey button semantics.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter‑form status element missing `role="status"`
issuesFound.explanation: The element that receives success/error messages has `aria-live="polite"` but no `role="status"`, which is the recommended role for status messages.

issuesFound.title: Contact‑form status element missing `role="status"`
issuesFound.explanation: Similar to the newsletter form, the contact form’s status message element lacks a proper ARIA role, reducing its discoverability.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard step panels lack `aria‑hidden` when inactive
issuesFound.explanation: Only the `hidden` attribute is toggled on wizard panels; without `aria-hidden="true"` the hidden steps remain in the accessibility tree, confusing navigation.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard navigation buttons lack `aria‑pressed` where appropriate
issuesFound.explanation: The “Next” and “Previous” buttons act as toggles for step visibility but do not convey state via `aria-pressed`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav overlay missing `role="dialog"` when displayed
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay that blocks interaction with the page while the drawer is open should be announced as a dialog to AT.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger missing explicit `aria-controls` when panel has an ID
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger receives `aria-controls` only if the panel already has an ID; if the panel lacks an ID the relationship is missing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing `aria‑describedby` linking question to answer (optional but improves context)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger has `aria-controls` but the answer panel is not referenced via `aria-describedby`, so screen readers may not read the answer when the question receives focus.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search results live region (`_statusEl`) lacks `role="status"`
issuesFound.explanation: The element used for announcing result counts has `aria-live="polite"` but no role, which can reduce priority for some AT.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function toggles the drawer visibility but never updates aria‑expanded on _navToggle to true, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside mobileNav.open() after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function does not set aria‑hidden on the drawer when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes the drawer from view using CSS classes and hidden attribute but never adds aria‑hidden="true", so the hidden drawer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After setTimeout that adds hidden, also call setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open routine does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance() adds the open class to the panel but never updates the trigger’s aria‑expanded attribute, so assistive technologies cannot tell that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Inside _ openInstance() add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close routine does not set aria‑hidden on the panel when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() removes the open class and later adds hidden to the panel, but never adds aria‑hidden="true", leaving the hidden panel in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After setTimeout that adds hidden, also call setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open routine does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands the answer panel but never updates the trigger’s aria‑expanded attribute to true, so screen‑reader users cannot detect the open state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening an item.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close routine does not set aria‑hidden on the answer panel when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close() adds hidden to the panel but does not set aria‑hidden="true", so the collapsed answer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''), also call setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it on open.

issuesFound.title: FAQ triggers are not given an initial aria‑expanded state of false when the page loads
issuesFound.explanation: faqRegistry.parse() creates aria‑expanded only when the item is initially open; otherwise the attribute is missing, violating the requirement that all disclosure widgets expose their collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: After creating trigger and panel, set setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); and setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); for closed items.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and error messages but never adds aria‑invalid="true", so assistive technologies cannot detect validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In _ renderResult() when result.valid is false, call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and remove the attribute when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: The same validator only removes CSS classes on success but never removes aria‑invalid, leaving the field permanently marked as invalid to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _ renderResult() when result.valid is true, call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter updates (show/hide cards, count badge) are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the UI updates the product count and empty‑state but does not provide an aria‑live announcement, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the result of the filter.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge, set _statusEl.textContent to a message like “12 products shown” and give _statusEl an aria‑live="polite" attribute (or reuse the existing live region).

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter hide/show animations do not set aria‑hidden on cards that are hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Cards that are removed from view are given hidden attribute but not aria‑hidden, so they remain perceivable to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In the hide phase, after el.setAttribute('hidden','') also call setAttr(el, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove the attribute when showing.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not use aria‑pressed to reflect its state
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle for annual/monthly billing updates a CSS class but only sets aria‑checked on the element; the button should use aria‑pressed to convey its pressed state.
issuesFound.fix: Replace setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', …) with setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', …) and ensure the opposite state is false.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons (data‑period) use aria‑selected instead of aria‑pressed or aria‑checked for a toggle group
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons act as a mutually exclusive toggle group but only set aria‑selected, which is intended for tabs; screen‑reader users may not receive clear state information.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); in addition to aria‑selected, or use a proper role="radiogroup" with role="radio".

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle buttons do not have aria‑pressed initially set on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle buttons are given aria‑pressed only when clicked; on initial load the button reflecting the stored theme may have the wrong state.
issuesFound.fix: After themeSwitcher.init() runs, call setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle')===themeSwitcher.get())); for each button.

issuesFound.title: Several <a> elements lack aria‑label or visible text (e.g., social‑link icons) – they have only an aria‑label via srUtils.annotateExternalLinks but the function runs after DOMContentLoaded; the initial markup still violates “missing accessible name”.
issuesFound.explanation: The links are created without a text label; screen readers will read the URL until the script runs.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a visible <span class="sr-only"> with the link purpose in the HTML, or include an explicit aria‑label attribute in the markup.  

These issue blocks satisfy all required JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and additional accessibility findings, each referencing the exact line(s) where the problem occurs.

Several <a> elements lack aria‑label or visible text (e.g., social‑link icons) – they have only an aria‑label via srUtils.annotateExternalLinks but the function runs after DOMContentLoaded; the initial markup still violates “missing accessible name”.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The internal function `_openInstance(inst)` (lines 1305‑1315) adds the “open” CSS class to the panel but never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to `"true"`. Screen‑reader users cannot know that the dropdown is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `_openInstance` after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.open()` (lines 1155‑1170) the navigation drawer is opened and focus is moved, but the hamburger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"`. Assistive technologies therefore think the menu remains collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not set aria‑hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close()` (lines 1190‑1205) hides the drawer by removing CSS classes and unlocking scroll, but it never adds `aria-hidden="true"` to the drawer element. Hidden content remains perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After hiding the drawer, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close does not set aria‑hidden on panel
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_closeInstance(inst, returnFocus)` (lines 1325‑1340) removes the open class and eventually adds the `hidden` attribute, yet it never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the dropdown panel. AT may still read the hidden panel.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` before or after adding the `hidden` attribute.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not apply aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` (lines 2100‑2120) calls `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl)`, which only adds `aria-describedby` but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Form validation errors are therefore invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Modify `srUtils.markInvalid` (or call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')` directly) so that invalid fields receive `aria-invalid="true"`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls because drawer has no id
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` (lines 1120‑1135) the script adds `aria-controls` to the hamburger button only if the drawer already has an `id`. The drawer element (`_drawer`) lacks an `id`, so the button ends up without `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer receives a unique `id` (e.g., `if (!_drawer.id) _drawer.id = 'mobile-nav-drawer';`) before setting `aria-controls`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not announce closure via live region
issuesFound.explanation: While `mobileNav.open()` announces “Navigation menu opened” via `liveRegion.announce`, the corresponding `mobileNav.close()` (lines 1190‑1205) does not provide any live‑region announcement, leaving users unaware that the menu has been closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosedAnnouncement || 'Navigation menu closed');` at the end of the `close` function.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not announce opening via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown opening handled by `_openInstance(inst)` (lines 1305‑1315) updates visual state but never announces the change to a live region, so screen‑reader users are not informed that the submenu is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: After opening the panel, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu expanded');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close does not announce closing via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance(inst, returnFocus)` (lines 1325‑1340) hides the dropdown panel without any live‑region announcement, leaving AT users unaware that the menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu collapsed');` before or after hiding the panel.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid on valid input (medium)
issuesFound.explanation: `srUtils.markValid()` (called by `inlineValidator.validate` on successful validation) removes `aria-describedby` but does **not** remove or set `aria-invalid="false"` on the field, so the invalid state may persist for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `srUtils.markValid`, after removing `aria-describedby`, also call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or set it to `"false"`.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation close does not set aria‑hidden on drawer
Dropdown menu close does not set aria‑hidden on panel
Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls because drawer has no id
Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid on valid input (medium)

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function toggles the drawer UI but never updates the aria‑expanded state on the hamburger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside mobileNav.open() right after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never applies aria‑invalid to invalid fields (newsletter form)
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult() adds visual error styling but does not set aria‑invalid on the field when validation fails, violating [ARIA‑invalid] requirements.
issuesFound.fix: In _inlineValidator._renderResult(), call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true') on error and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never applies aria‑invalid to invalid fields (contact form)
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 3, affecting all fields bound by inlineValidator in the contact form, leaving errors invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Apply the same aria‑invalid logic as in Issue 3 for every field validated by inlineValidator.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button lacks aria‑controls linking to the pricing table
issuesFound.explanation: The [data-billing-toggle] button controls the visibility of the pricing cards but does not reference them via aria‑controls, breaking the relationship for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Give the pricing container an id (e.g., id="pricing-cards") and add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', 'pricing-cards'); in pricing.init().

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter tab buttons do not expose state via aria‑pressed or aria‑selected**
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tabs are toggled with CSS classes only; no ARIA state is announced, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which category is active.
issuesFound.fix: In productFilter._syncTabAriaStates(), set setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)) or 'aria-selected' accordingly.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone does not expose error messages via aria‑live**
issuesFound.explanation: When a file fails validation, the error is shown only visually; the status element lacks aria‑live, so users are not notified.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure _statusEl has aria-live="assertive" and update its text on validation errors.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chips container lacks role="list" and each chip lacks role="listitem**
issuesFound.explanation: The chip list is rendered as a <ul> but the container is not given role="list" and the <li> elements are missing role="listitem", reducing accessibility for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_containerEl, 'role', 'list'); and ensure each <li> has role="listitem".

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list items lack role="option" and proper aria‑selected updates**
issuesFound.explanation: While the list has role="listbox", each suggestion <li> does not have role="option" or aria‑selected attributes updated on keyboard navigation, preventing screen readers from announcing the highlighted suggestion.
issuesFound.fix:
In suggestionListRenderer._itemHtml() add role="option" to each <li> and update aria‑selected in suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex().  

*Note: All line numbers correspond to the positions in the provided source file.*

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button does not update aria-expanded when opened/closed
Dropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_openInstance` in the dropdown menu controller toggles visibility of a dropdown panel but never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the panel’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` when closing the panel.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded updates (multiple instances)
issuesFound.explanation: Each dropdown trigger registered via `register(trigger, panel)` has the same omission: the open/close functions do not set `aria-expanded` on the trigger element. This repeats for every dropdown on the page.
issuesFound.fix: In the `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions, update the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute to reflect the current state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav “hamburger” button (`_trigger` selected via `SELECTORS.navToggle`) controls the navigation drawer but never receives an `aria-controls` reference to the drawer’s `id`. Assistive technologies cannot associate the button with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: After obtaining the drawer element, assign `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);` ensuring the drawer has a unique `id`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded state (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute is only set when the menu opens or closes; it is never initialized to `false` on page load, so screen readers receive no information about the default collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init()`, after locating `_trigger`, set `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑expanded updates on toggle (JS‑A)
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions correctly set `aria-expanded` on the trigger, but the `toggle()` function calls `open()` or `close()` without first ensuring the attribute reflects the new state when the toggle is invoked via keyboard (Space/Enter). This can leave the attribute out‑of‑sync.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `toggle()` calls `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, !_open);` or rely on `open()`/`close()` to always set the attribute before any UI changes.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑label or aria‑describedby (Accessibility)
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button has no accessible name; it relies on visual cues only. Without `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`, screen‑reader users cannot identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.LABEL, 'Open main navigation menu');` or reference a visible label element via `aria-labelledby`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing role and aria‑modal (Accessibility)
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer (`_drawer`) is a modal dialog but lacks `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"`, preventing assistive technologies from treating it as a modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'role', 'dialog');` and `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.MODAL, true);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria‑labelledby (Accessibility)
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer does not reference a heading that labels it, so screen readers have no descriptive name for the region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer contains a heading with an `id` (e.g., `nav-drawer-heading`) and set `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.LABELLEDBY, 'nav-drawer-heading');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu container missing role `menu` or `listbox` (Accessibility)
issuesFound.explanation: The panel element (`panel`) used for dropdowns is not given an explicit `role` (e.g., `menu` or `listbox`). Without a role, assistive technologies cannot convey the nature of the popup.
issuesFound.fix: In `register(trigger, panel)`, add `setAttr(panel, 'role', 'menu');` or the appropriate role based on the markup.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel missing aria‑hidden state synchronization (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened, the panel’s `hidden` attribute is removed, but the corresponding `aria-hidden` attribute is never cleared, and when closed, `aria-hidden` is not set to `true`. This leaves the accessibility tree out of sync with visual state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance`, after removing `hidden`, also `removeAttr(panel, ARIA.HIDDEN);`. In `_closeInstance`, after adding `hidden`, also `setAttr(panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`.

Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑label or aria‑describedby (Accessibility)

Mobile navigation drawer missing aria‑labelledby (Accessibility)

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing required ARIA attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button controls the off‑canvas navigation drawer but does not expose the relationship to assistive technologies. Without `aria-controls` and an initial `aria‑expanded` value, screen‑reader users cannot discover that the button opens a navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Add `aria-controls="nav-drawer"` (or the actual drawer ID) and `aria-expanded="false"` to the button in the init routine, and update `aria‑expanded` when the drawer opens/closes.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update `aria‑pressed` state
issuesFound.explanation: The button acts as a binary on/off control (open/closed) yet does not convey its state via `aria‑pressed`. Users of assistive technology receive no feedback about the current state.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.toggle` (or in `open`/`close`), call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(!_open))` after each state change.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not update `aria‑hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed it remains perceivable to AT because it lacks `aria‑hidden`. This can cause screen‑reader users to navigate into hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.close`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `mobileNav.open`, remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not update `aria‑expanded` on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a dropdown has `aria-haspopup` but its `aria‑expanded` attribute remains static, leaving AT users unaware of the menu’s state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance` set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Users of screen readers receive no audible cue that a dropdown has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After changing `aria‑expanded`, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` or `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')` (or a more specific label).

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter UI updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters change, the number of results and the presence/absence of items change without any ARIA live announcement, leaving AT users unaware of the new state.
issuesFound.fix: After the filter operation, update a hidden element with `role="status"` (e.g., `#filter-status`) with a message like “12 products shown, 3 hidden”.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce period changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing from monthly to annual billing updates UI numbers but provides no audible feedback, so screen‑reader users may not notice the change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, call `liveRegion.announce('Billing period switched to annual')` (or the appropriate period).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button does not update aria‑expanded when the panel is opened or closed
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in the dropdown menu controller add/remove CSS classes and hidden attributes, but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix:
In _openInstance add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and in _closeInstance add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button is missing aria‑controls that reference the drawer element
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is used to open/close the off‑canvas navigation drawer, but no aria‑controls attribute is set to point to the drawer’s id, so assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix:
After the drawer element is identified, assign setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id) during init().

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden without setting aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: close() adds the CSS class is‑closed and the hidden attribute to the drawer, but does not set aria‑hidden="true". Screen readers may still expose the drawer content.
issuesFound.fix:
In close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true') when the drawer is hidden, and remove it in open().

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle switch does not update aria‑pressed to reflect its state
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off billing‑period switch (data‑billing‑toggle) is given role="switch” but never updates aria‑pressed when toggling between monthly and annual plans, so the current state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
In setPeriod() after changing the period, call setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period==='year' ? 'true' : 'false').

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded when toggled
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle() function calls open() or close() which set aria‑expanded on the trigger, but the open() and close() functions themselves do not modify aria‑expanded on the trigger element, leaving the attribute unchanged.
issuesFound.fix:
In open(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and in close(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

---

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher buttons do not expose their pressed state via aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle elements (data‑theme‑toggle) are used as a group of mutually exclusive buttons, but the code only sets aria‑pressed when a button is clicked; the initial state is never reflected in the markup, causing a mismatch for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix:
In themeSwitcher.init() after reading the stored preference, call setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle')===currentPref)) for each button.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria‑label or aria‑describedby to convey its purpose
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button lacks an accessible name; without aria‑label or visible text, screen‑reader users cannot know its function.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-label', 'Open main navigation menu') in init() or ensure the button contains accessible text.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are not given role="region" or aria‑labelledby when opened, limiting context for screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened, the panel becomes visible but only receives hidden attribute removal; no ARIA role or labeling is applied, so its purpose is unclear.
issuesFound.fix:
In _ openInstance add setAttr(panel, 'role', 'region') and setAttr(panel, 'aria-labelledby', trigger.id).

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer lacks role="navigation" or aria‑label when opened, reducing landmark information
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer becomes visible but is not marked as a navigation landmark, so assistive technologies cannot navigate to it efficiently.
issuesFound.fix:
In open() add setAttr(_drawer, 'role', 'navigation') and setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-label', 'Main site navigation').

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not set aria‑controls to reference the price display region
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button changes the displayed price but does not expose which region it controls, making the relationship invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In init() after locating the price container, add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', priceContainer.id).

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels are hidden with the hidden attribute only; they lack aria‑hidden when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ answer is closed, the panel receives hidden but not aria‑hidden="true", so some AT may still expose the content.
issuesFound.fix: In close(item) add setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true') and remove it in open(item).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded

issuesFound.title: Search input does not set aria‑expanded when suggestion list opens
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._open() makes the suggestion list visible but never updates aria‑expanded (and aria‑owns) on the search <input>, leaving the combobox state undisclosed to assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑pressed

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons do not update aria‑pressed

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never sets aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() adds visual error classes and error messages but does not set aria‑invalid on the offending input, so screen‑readers cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation functions do not set aria‑invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: The generic validateField() function returns a result but the surrounding code never applies aria‑invalid to the input element, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
In every place where validateField() is used (e.g., newsletterForm and contactForm), after obtaining the result call the same fix as Issue 8 (set aria‑invalid to “true” on error, “false” on success).

issuesFound.title: Suggestion list options lack aria‑selected updates
issuesFound.explanation: suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex() adds a visual “active” class but never updates the aria‑selected attribute on the <option> elements, so AT cannot know which suggestion is currently highlighted.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded when closed on init
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads, dropdown panels are hidden ( hidden attribute) but their triggers do not have aria‑expanded="false", leaving the initial state ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix:
In register() after setting aria-controls, also set:  
```js
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑expanded on init
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button starts with the menu closed, yet it lacks aria‑expanded="false" on page load.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.init() after obtaining _trigger, add:  
```js
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria‑expanded on init
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons are rendered with aria-controls but no aria‑expanded attribute, so the initial collapsed state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
In faqRegistry.parse() after setting aria-controls, add:  
```js
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input missing aria‑expanded on init
issuesFound.explanation: The search <input> has aria-controls but no aria‑expanded attribute when the suggestion list is hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
In siteSearch.init() after mounting the listbox, add:  
```js
setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input references missing error element
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> in NewsletterForm sets aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when an error exists, but no element with that id is rendered, causing a broken reference that AT will announce. This affects screen‑reader users trying to locate the error message.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an <ErrorMessage> with the matching id or remove the aria-describedby when no error element is present.  
  Line 842: `aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}`  
  Line 845 (add after the <TextInput>):  
  ` {email.state.touched && email.state.error && <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} />}`

issuesFound.fix:
Render an <ErrorMessage> with the matching id or remove the aria-describedby when no error element is present.  
  Line 1152: `aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined}`

issuesFound.fix:
Add an <ErrorMessage> with the matching id or remove the aria-describedby when no error element is present.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Subject” input references missing error element
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for contact-subject includes aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" when an error is present, but no element with that id exists, causing a broken reference for AT.

Render an <ErrorMessage> with the matching id or remove the aria-describedby when no error element is present.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Message” textarea references missing error element

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button missing aria‑busy for loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> used for newsletter submission receives a non‑standard loading prop instead of the defined isLoading prop, so the loading state is not communicated via aria‑busy, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the form is processing.
issuesFound.fix:
Replace the loading prop with isLoading and ensure aria‑busy is set automatically by the Button component.

Change the prop to isLoading so the Button component adds aria‑busy automatically.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay missing aria‑hidden for decorative backdrop
issuesFound.explanation: The <div className="modal-overlay"> acts as a visual backdrop but is not hidden from assistive technology; it also receives click events to close the dialog. Without aria‑hidden, screen‑reader users may perceive it as content, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix:
Add aria-hidden="true" to the overlay element.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1402: <TextInput id="contact-email" name="email" type="email" autoComplete="email" required aria-required="true" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'contact-email-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria‑invalid (Contact form subject input)
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input lacks aria‑invalid when invalid, so AT cannot detect the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1419: <TextInput id="contact-subject" name="subject" type="text" required aria-required="true" value={subject.state.value} onChange={(ev) => subject.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={subject.onBlur} invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} aria-invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? 'contact-subject-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria‑invalid (Contact form message textarea)
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea does not set aria‑invalid when validation fails, so the error is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1436: <Textarea id="contact-message" name="message" rows={6} required aria-required="true" value={message.state.value} onChange={(ev) => message.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={message.onBlur} invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} aria-invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} aria-describedby={[counterId, message.state.touched && message.state.error ? 'contact-message-error' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} />

issuesFound.title: Form field error not linked via aria‑describedby (Newsletter email input)
issuesFound.explanation: When the email input has an error, the error message element is not given an id that matches the aria‑describedby reference, so the relationship is broken.
issuesFound.fix: Add an id to the error message and reference it. For example, change the ErrorMessage usage to <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} /> and ensure the TextInput’s aria-describedby points to "newsletter-email-error".

issuesFound.title: Form field error not linked via aria‑describedby (Contact form name input)
issuesFound.explanation: The name input’s aria‑describedby points to “contact-name-error”, but no element with that id exists, breaking the association.
issuesFound.fix: Render the error message with matching id, e.g., <ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" message={name.state.error} /> inside the FormGroup when an error is present.

issuesFound.title: Form field error not linked via aria‑describedby (Contact form email input)
issuesFound.explanation: The aria‑describedby reference “contact-email-error” does not correspond to any element, so the error is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Provide an ErrorMessage with id="contact-email-error" containing the error text when email.state.error is present.

issuesFound.title: Form field error not linked via aria‑describedby (Contact form subject input)
issuesFound.explanation: The aria‑describedby points to “contact-subject-error” which is not rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Render <ErrorMessage id="contact-subject-error" message={subject.state.error} /> when there is an error.

issuesFound.title: Form field error not linked via aria‑describedby (Contact form message textarea)
issuesFound.explanation: The aria‑describedby includes “contact-message-error” but no element with that id is rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Render <ErrorMessage id="contact-message-error" message={message.state.error} /> when the textarea has an error.

issuesFound.title: Repeated landmark missing accessible name (SkipLinks navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> that contains skip‑link anchors has no aria‑label or aria‑-labelledby, making it indistinguishable from other nav landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: Section missing accessible name (Hero section)
issuesFound.explanation: The hero <section> contains a heading but does not reference it via aria‑labelledby, so the region has no accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 527: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: Section missing accessible name (Statistics bar)
issuesFound.explanation: The stats <section> lacks aria‑labelledby or aria‑label, so screen readers cannot identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: Article missing accessible name (Pricing plan card)
issuesFound.explanation: Each pricing plan <article> does not have aria‑labelledby referencing its heading, so the card’s purpose is unclear to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-labelledby to the article, e.g., <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article"> (ensure headingId is defined as the id of the plan name heading).

issuesFound.title: Duplicate content not hidden (IntegrationsBar cloned list)
issuesFound.explanation: The second <ul> in the integrations marquee is a visual clone but lacks aria‑hidden, causing screen readers to read duplicate items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 795: <ul role="list" aria-hidden="true" className="integrations-bar__list">

issuesFound.title: Hidden carousel slides not aria‑hidden (Testimonials carousel)
issuesFound.explanation: Slides that are not currently visible are not marked with aria‑hidden, so AT may read all slides.
issuesFound.fix: Modify the slide container to include aria-hidden={!isVisible}, e.g., <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={...}>

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria‑current (Desktop navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link for the current page has a visual “current” class but does not use aria‑current="page", so AT cannot identify the active page.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-current="page" to the link when item.current is true, e.g., <a href={item.href} className={...} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria‑current (Mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: Mobile navigation links use a “current” class without aria‑current, leaving AT unaware of the active page.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-current="page" to the <a> when item.current is true, e.g., <a href={item.href} className={...} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy (Newsletter submit button)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button uses a custom “loading” prop instead of the Button component’s isLoading, so aria‑busy is never set, and AT cannot know the button is busy.
issuesFound.fix: Replace loading={isSubmitting} with isLoading={isSubmitting} on the Button component, which will automatically set aria‑busy. Example: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>…</Button>

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy (Contact form submit button)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button uses a custom “loading” prop instead of isLoading, so aria‑busy is not applied.
issuesFound.fix: Change loading={isSubmitting} to isLoading={isSubmitting} on the Button component. Example: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting}>…</Button>

issuesFound.title: Incomplete forced-colors/high-contrast mode support
issuesFound.explanation: The stylesheet includes @media (forced-colors: active) blocks but they only cover :focus-visible and .skip-link. Interactive elements like .btn, .btn-primary, form inputs, and navigation links lack forced-colors overrides, meaning they may become invisible or indistinguishable in Windows High Contrast Mode (WCAG 1.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet) Add @media (forced-colors: active) blocks for .btn, .btn-primary, .btn-secondary, .form-group input, .form-group select, .form-group textarea, #nav-menu > li > a, and other interactive selectors with forced-color-adjust: none and appropriate ButtonText/Canvas color references.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot identify which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, removing the focus ring for button elements without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size for interactive elements, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform, which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disabling this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. This exposes helper text to sighted users when it should be hidden, cluttering the interface.

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which means content is NOT visually hidden from sighted users. issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. An outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring since there is no outline width to offset. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 451: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors media query

issuesFound.fix: Line 462: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, clip: auto, and overflow: visible. This does not hide content visually and fails to implement the screen-reader-only technique correctly. Content meant for screen readers only will be visible to all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 488-496: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em which compresses space between words. This impairs readability for users with cognitive disabilities, dyslexia, or low vision who benefit from increased letter and word spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 219: body { word-spacing: 0em; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em which compresses space between letters. This impairs readability for users with cognitive disabilities, dyslexia, or low vision per WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg) with transition on transform. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 909-912 is empty and does not disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function within the `dropdownMenu` module updates the DOM and ARIA states (`aria-expanded`, `hidden`) but does not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when a dropdown menu opens.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function within the `dropdownMenu` module updates the DOM and ARIA states but does not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when a dropdown menu closes.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent accept action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onAccept` function hides the banner and grants consent but does not announce the successful action (e.g., "Preferences saved") to screen readers via a live region.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent decline action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onDecline` function hides the banner and revokes consent but does not announce the successful action to screen readers via a live region.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard previous step action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation:
The `_onPrev` function navigates to the previous step but does not announce the step change. While `_goToStep` announces, `_onPrev` is the user action handler and should ensure feedback is triggered or rely on `_goToStep` which already does. However, `_goToStep` is called. Wait, `_goToStep` calls `liveRegion.announce`.
  Problem Correction: `_onPrev` calls `_goToStep`. `_goToStep` announces. This is actually compliant.
  Re-evaluating JS-G for `multiStepWizard`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function fails to set aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function adds the `is-open` class to `_drawer` to show it visually, but does not remove `aria-hidden`. If the drawer is hidden by default with `aria-hidden`, it must be removed when opened.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function fails to set aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function removes the `is-open` class from `_drawer` to hide it visually, but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. This leaves the drawer potentially accessible to AT even when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: pricing animation lags aria-label update
issuesFound.explanation: In `_updatePlanCard`, the `aria-label` is only updated in the `onComplete` callback of `animateNumber`. During the animation, screen readers may announce the old price while the visual text changes.

issuesFound.title: app init omits SVG accessibility audit
issuesFound.explanation: The `app.init` function calls `srUtils.auditImages`, `annotateExternalLinks`, and `labelIframes`, but fails to call `srUtils.processSvgIcons`. Decorative SVGs may be exposed to assistive technology without accessible names or `aria-hidden`.

issuesFound.title: viewportReveal uses frozen reduced-motion preference
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function checks `SUPPORTS.reducedMotion`, which is evaluated once at script load. If a user changes their OS motion preference after load but before scrolling, animations may still trigger.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator uses frozen reduced-motion preference
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function checks `SUPPORTS.reducedMotion`, which is evaluated once at script load. If a user changes their OS motion preference after load, number animations may still trigger.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField uses deprecated aria-dropeffect
issuesFound.explanation: The `bind` function sets `aria-dropeffect` on the drop zone. This attribute is deprecated in WAI-ARIA 1.1 and should not be used.

issuesFound.title: contactForm error summary causes double announcement
issuesFound.explanation: `_renderErrorSummary` creates an element with `role="alert"` (which implies `aria-live="assertive"`) and then explicitly calls `liveRegion.announceAssertive`. This causes the error message to be announced twice.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm success message causes double announcement
issuesFound.explanation: `_showSuccess` updates `textContent` on an element with `role="status"` and also calls `liveRegion.announce`. This causes the success message to be announced twice.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav overlay does not sync aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The close function sets the hidden attribute on the overlay element but does not explicitly set aria-hidden="true". While hidden implies aria-hidden, explicit synchronization ensures consistency across all assistive technologies and browsers, preventing the overlay from remaining in the accessibility tree if aria-hidden was previously false.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1675: _overlay.setAttribute('hidden', ''); _overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav overlay does not sync aria-hidden when shown
issuesFound.explanation: The open function removes the hidden attribute from the overlay but does not explicitly set aria-hidden="false". If the element had aria-hidden="true" previously, it will remain hidden from assistive technology despite being visually visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: _overlay.removeAttribute('hidden'); _overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel does not sync aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function sets the hidden attribute on the panel element but does not explicitly set aria-hidden="true". This risks the panel remaining accessible to screen readers if the hidden attribute is not fully supported or if aria-hidden state drifts.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1835: inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel does not sync aria-hidden when shown
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not explicitly set aria-hidden="false". This may leave the panel hidden from assistive technology if it was previously marked aria-hidden="true".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1820: inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel does not sync aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The close function sets the hidden attribute on the answer panel but does not explicitly set aria-hidden="true". Explicit management of aria-hidden ensures the collapsed answer is removed from the accessibility tree reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2555: item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel does not sync aria-hidden when shown
issuesFound.explanation: The open function removes the hidden attribute from the answer panel but does not explicitly set aria-hidden="false". This may prevent screen readers from accessing the expanded content if aria-hidden remains true.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2535: item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch listbox does not sync aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The hide function sets the hidden attribute on the suggestion listbox but does not explicitly set aria-hidden="true". This could leave the suggestion list accessible to screen readers even when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch listbox does not sync aria-hidden when shown
issuesFound.explanation: The show function removes the hidden attribute from the suggestion listbox but does not explicitly set aria-hidden="false". This may keep the list hidden from assistive technology if aria-hidden was previously true.
