issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` helper function removes the error description but does not remove the `aria-invalid` attribute. Fields that were previously invalid will remain announced as invalid to assistive technologies even after correction.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance/_closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu` module sets the initial `aria-expanded="false"` in `register`, but the toggle functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` modify the panel visibility without updating the trigger's `aria-expanded` attribute. The ARIA state becomes out of sync with the visual state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function expands the answer panel and updates internal state, but it fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger. The `close` function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state management where the expanded state is not reflected in ARIA.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters` function updates the product grid, count badge, and empty state dynamically. While it emits a custom event, it does not announce the result count or filter change to screen readers via an `aria-live` region, leaving users unaware that the content has updated.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function updates all price displays on the page when the billing period changes. This significant content update occurs without an `aria-live` announcement, so screen reader users are not informed that the prices have changed.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `siteSearch` module mounts the suggestion listbox and wires ARIA attributes like `aria-controls`, but it does not explicitly set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the search input. The state is only managed when the list opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop._onClick lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll-to-top button moves the viewport and focus, but does not announce the action. While focus movement helps, an explicit confirmation ("Scrolled to top") ensures users understand the context change, especially if focus management is delayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line scrollToTop._onClick: setTimeout(() => { returnEl.focus({ preventScroll: true }); liveRegion.announce('Scrolled back to top of page'); }, 300);

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The consent banner is dynamically injected into the DOM and focus is moved to it, but no announcement is made that a dialog has appeared. Users might be confused by the sudden focus change without context.

issuesFound.title: planComparisonHighlighter.highlightColumn lacks announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Hovering or clicking column headers in the pricing comparison table highlights a column and updates `aria-selected`, but the change is not announced. Screen reader users navigating the table headers will not know which plan is currently highlighted.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator._animate lacks final value announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The statistic numbers animate from 0 to the target value. While the final `aria-label` is set on completion, the change is not announced via a live region. Users may miss the updated statistic entirely if they are not focused on that element.

fpTitles: cookieConsent._showBanner lacks live region announcement
statCounterAnimator._animate lacks final value announcement

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not updated
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the `aria-expanded` state on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is open or closed when toggling via the button.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1673: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1695: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger aria-expanded not updated
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` functions show and hide the dropdown panel but do not update the `aria-expanded` state on the `inst.trigger` button. The trigger's expanded state remains stale (initially 'false'), misleading assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1828: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1845: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge not announced as live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._updateCountBadge` function updates the text content of `_countBadge` to reflect the number of visible products, but the element lacks an `aria-live` attribute. Screen reader users are not automatically announced when the product count changes after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2633: setAttr(_countBadge, ARIA.LIVE, 'polite');

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message not announced as live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing._updateSavingsMessage` function updates the text content of `_savingsMsg` to show discount information when the billing period changes, but the element lacks an `aria-live` attribute. This dynamic update is not announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2963: setAttr(_savingsMsg, ARIA.LIVE, 'polite');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function expands the answer panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `item.trigger`. While the `close` function correctly sets it to "false", the state becomes inconsistent when opening items dynamically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3233: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion filtered items not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion._filterByQuery` function hides non-matching FAQ items by setting the `hidden` attribute but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. Depending on browser/AT combinations, these items may remain in the accessibility tree despite being visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3345: setAttr(item.el, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');
  Line 3342: item.el.removeAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN);

issuesFound.title: srUtils validation functions missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` and `srUtils.markValid` functions manage error descriptions but do not set or remove the `aria-invalid` attribute on the `field`. Assistive technologies rely on `aria-invalid="true"` to announce that a field has an error.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1463: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');
  Line 1477: field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` function adds an error description via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Screen readers will announce the description but will not identify the field as invalid, causing users to miss the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2375: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Valid state resets do not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid` function removes the error description but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"` on the field. If a field was previously invalid, it remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2393: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function toggles the navigation drawer visibility and body classes but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1270: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation missing focus trap initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module declares a `_trap` variable but never initializes it with `createFocusTrap` in the `init` function. When the mobile menu opens, keyboard focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing users to tab into obscured page content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1335: _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { escapeDeactivates: true });

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function changes the visible state of the navigation but does not announce this change to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on AT may not realize the menu has opened.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden dropdown panel not updated in accessibility tree

issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open, close, and toggle functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not know if the menu is open or closed.

'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: Touch target at minimum but below recommended size on footer navigation links

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause motion sensitivity issues. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists for this selector.
issuesFound.fix: Line 575: (add after existing transitions) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card has a transform animation on hover (transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01)) that is not covered by the existing reduced-motion media query, which only targets .pricing-card:hover.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1564: (add to existing media query at line 1570) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline. This removes the focus indicator entirely for keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA).

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus:not(:focus-visible) selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. While this targets mouse focus, it removes the default focus ring with no replacement style, and when paired with the broken :focus-visible rule, leaves no focus indicator for any input mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only outline-offset: 3px without a visible box-shadow or border replacement. This removes the focus indicator for buttons when navigated via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the recommended 44px target size (WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size - AA). While it meets the minimum 24px requirement, the small size may still be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 400:
  ```css
  min-height: 44px;
  ```

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the recommended 44px target size for AA compliance, potentially causing difficulty for users with tremors or limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 478:

  min-height: 44px;
  ```

issuesFound.fix:

  ```

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide content. The content remains visible in the layout while intended to be screen-reader only, or may not be properly hidden. The class should use position: absolute, 1px dimensions, and clip/clip-path.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and defines an active state with transform: translateY(1px) that causes spatial movement. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override is provided for this animation, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class defines a transform transition (transition: transform var(--transition-base)) that causes spatial movement when the link slides into view. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override disables this movement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The custom property --colour-text-secondary is set to #aaaaaa, which on the white background (#ffffff) produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum) for normal text (requires 4.5:1) and for large text (requires 3:1).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 50:
  ```css
  --colour-text-secondary:   #767676;   /* 4.5:1 contrast on white */
  ```

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The selector nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or consistent font-weight bold) in the default state. This causes the links to rely solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer bottom links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .footer-bottom > p a sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator, relying solely on colour to identify the link.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or a non-zero outline width. This removes the keyboard focus indicator entirely, making it impossible for keyboard-only users to see which element has focus. The outline-offset property alone does not create a visible ring.

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. This removes the focus indicator from buttons when navigated via keyboard, violating WCAG 2.4.11. The outline-offset property requires a visible outline width to function.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended functionality of the utility class.

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property (transition: transform var(--transition-fast)), which causes spatial movement. issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector removes the outline entirely (outline: none) and provides only outline-offset. In high-contrast modes, this eliminates the focus indicator completely, preventing users from seeing which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector suppresses the default focus outline with outline: none and provides only outline-offset. Despite the comment claiming a 3 px solid ring is present, the actual code renders no visible focus indicator for buttons, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the visible focus ring. Users navigating via keyboard cannot see when the search button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector removes the outline (outline: none) and relies solely on outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus indicator for these links.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24 px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (AA). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small button without accidentally hitting adjacent elements.

  min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24 px minimum touch target size. Icon-only buttons are frequently used for critical actions, making undersized targets a significant barrier.

issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, resulting in a 28 px touch target. This meets the 24 px minimum but falls below the 44 px recommended size, making activation difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from layout
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text becomes visible in the layout, breaking the visual design, while the incorrect properties may cause content to be announced incorrectly or not at all.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), enabling spatial movement animations. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists for this selector, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (AAA) and potentially triggering vestibular disorders.

This 3D rotation animation lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override, which can cause nausea or disorientation for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class sets outline: none with only an outline-offset change, failing to provide a visible focus indicator for keyboard users. The negative offset alone does not render a visible ring when the outline style is none.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets only outline-color without restoring outline-style, leaving the outline as none. This results in no visible focus indicator for keyboard users despite the intent to show a brand-colored ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, select:focus-visible, textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the outline on focus-visible without replacement, relying solely on border-color change which may not be sufficient for keyboard users to identify focused elements, particularly in high-contrast modes or for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs have no visible focus indicator, making it impossible for keyboard users to determine when the checkbox is focused and can be toggled with the spacebar.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the footer brand section remove the focus outline without replacement, preventing keyboard users from identifying when these links are focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links lack a visible focus indicator, making it difficult for keyboard users to navigate the footer links sequentially.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links in the footer remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, leaving keyboard users without indication of focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons have min-width and min-height of 20px, failing the 24px minimum requirement. These buttons are often used for critical actions like close or menu toggles and must be large enough to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes have explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes them difficult to toggle for users with tremors or limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button component includes transform in its transition properties, which can cause motion-induced vestibular issues. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transform transition for users who have requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform transitions and 3D rotation on hover, which can trigger vestibular disorders. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 706-709 is empty and fails to disable these animations.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words below the default. This can cause words to visually run together, making text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: required-marker
issuesFound.explanation: The required field marker uses var(--colour-error) (#ee9999) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text, making the required indicator difficult to see.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: section-header paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The section-header paragraph uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background, providing only 2.6:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA for normal text and makes descriptive content hard to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer-brand paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The footer brand paragraph uses var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against the footer background var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555), resulting in approximately 2.6:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA and makes footer description text illegible.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer-brand address
issuesFound.explanation: The address text uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against #555555 background, resulting in approximately 1.6:1 contrast. This is well below the 4.5:1 requirement and makes contact information unreadable for many users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on #555555 background (2.6:1 contrast), failing WCAG AA. Users cannot easily distinguish these links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer-bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: The footer copyright text uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against #555555, providing only 1.6:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA for text visibility.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer-bottom links
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the footer bottom use var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against #555555 (2.6:1 contrast), failing WCAG AA. These links are indistinguishable from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: social-links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links use var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against the #555555 footer background, resulting in 2.6:1 contrast. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for text links.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative: footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change on hover without an underline or other non-color differentiator. This makes links indistinguishable from surrounding text for colorblind users, violating WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative: social-links
issuesFound.explanation: Social links remove underlines via text-decoration: none and only change color on hover, providing no non-color visual indicator. Colorblind users cannot distinguish these links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and provides only outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA) as there is no box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline replacement.

issuesFound.fix: Line 930: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons set outline: none without replacement, making focus invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1019: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table container removes focus outline without replacement, hiding keyboard focus from users navigating the region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1044: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove focus outline without providing a visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from seeing selection state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1088: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove focus outline without replacement, hiding keyboard focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove focus outline without replacement, preventing keyboard users from tracking focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1161: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove focus outline without replacement, hiding focus from keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 575: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons set min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size and making them difficult to tap accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 587: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes explicitly size to 20px by 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size, making them difficult for users with motor impairments to select accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1222: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and width: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout and does not hide it from visual users. Screen-reader-only text will be displayed to all users, breaking the design and potentially confusing sighted users, while also failing to provide the intended screen-reader-only functionality.
issuesFound.fix: Line 386-396: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button component includes transform in its transition property and applies transform: translateY(1px) on :active. Users with vestibular disorders who prefer reduced motion will still see the button move when activated because no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists for this transform.
issuesFound.fix: Line 494: Add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform properties that create a 3D perspective effect. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see this animation because the empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 898 does not override the transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 888: Add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state removes the 3D transform (rotateY/rotateX). Without a reduced-motion override, users with motion sensitivity will still experience the spatial animation when hovering.
issuesFound.fix: Line 893: Add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) on hover. This spatial movement lacks a reduced-motion override, potentially affecting users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: Add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: Skip links use transform: translateY(-200%) and transition: transform for their reveal animation. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the slide animation because no override exists.
issuesFound.fix: Line 415: Add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button includes transform in its transition and applies transform on hover. This spatial animation lacks a reduced-motion override for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 856: Add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which tightens the space between words significantly. This reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities, dyslexia, or low vision who rely on clear word boundaries.
issuesFound.fix: Line 318: body { ... word-spacing: 0em; ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on code element
issuesFound.explanation: The code element uses color: var(--colour-brand-secondary) (#bb88ee) against background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100) (#f1f5f9). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.7:1, failing the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text (14px medium weight).
issuesFound.fix: Line 383: code { background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100); color: var(--colour-neutral-800); padding: var(--space-0-5) var(--space-1-5); border-radius: var(--radius-base); border: 1px solid var(--colour-neutral-200); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .skip-link

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor reset sets text-decoration: none on all links without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline on hover/focus or border-bottom). Sighted users cannot distinguish links from surrounding text without hovering over them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 324: Remove the global text-decoration: none reset and apply text-decoration: none only to specific component classes that provide alternative visual indicators (e.g., buttons or nav items with background highlights).

issuesFound.title: Contact form first-name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form's first-name input field (selected via '#contact-first-name') collects personal data but does not programmatically declare its purpose via the autocomplete attribute. Screen-reader users and password managers cannot reliably identify this as a given-name field, violating WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: Line: In the contactForm.init() method where CONTACT_FIELDS are bound, add autocomplete attribute: const field = qs(cfg.selector, _formEl); if (field) { field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'given-name'); inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules); }

issuesFound.title: Contact form last-name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The last-name input (selected via '#contact-last-name') lacks an autocomplete attribute. Without autocomplete="family-name", assistive technologies cannot programmatically determine that this field expects a surname, failing WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In contactForm.init(), after selecting the field: field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'family-name');

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The email input (selected via '#contact-email') does not expose autocomplete="email". This prevents browsers and assistive technologies from recognizing the field as an email address input, violating WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In contactForm.init(), after selecting the field: field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription email missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form's email input (selected via '#newsletter-email') collects an email address but omits the autocomplete="email" attribute. This prevents proper programmatic identification of the field's purpose per WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In newsletterForm.init(), after selecting _emailEl: _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');

issuesFound.title: Wizard full-name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard's name input (name="wizard_name") collects the user's full name but lacks autocomplete="name". This prevents assistive technologies from programmatically determining the field's purpose, violating WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In multiStepWizard.init() where STEP_FIELDS are bound, add: field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');

issuesFound.title: Wizard email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard's email field (name="wizard_email") does not declare autocomplete="email", preventing programmatic identification of the input's purpose and violating WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In multiStepWizard.init(), add: field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');

issuesFound.title: Wizard cardholder name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The cardholder name field (name="wizard_card_name") lacks autocomplete="cc-name", preventing browsers and assistive technologies from recognizing this as a credit-card name field per WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In multiStepWizard.init(), add: field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'cc-name');

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs incorrectly convey state using aria-pressed instead of aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as tab controls (selecting a view) but use aria-pressed to indicate the active state. Tabs must use aria-selected="true|false" and role="tab" to correctly convey their single-select nature to screen readers. Using aria-pressed causes assistive technologies to announce these as toggle buttons rather than tabs, violating WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line: In _syncTabAriaStates(), replace setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)) with setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)); and ensure each button has role="tab" by adding if (!btn.getAttribute('role')) btn.setAttribute('role', 'tab');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer remains in accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed via mobileNav.close(), the code removes CSS classes but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the _drawer element. If the CSS does not use display:none (e.g., uses transform off-screen), the drawer remains perceivable to screen readers. Conversely, open() does not remove aria-hidden. This violates WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as hidden content must be removed from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.close(), after removeClass(_drawer, ...): setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); In mobileNav.open(), after addClass(_drawer, ...): _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');

issuesFound.title: Character counter not programmatically associated with input field
issuesFound.explanation: The character counter creates a live region to announce character counts, but the counter element is not associated with the input via aria-describedby. Screen-reader users navigating to the input will not know that a character limit exists until they begin typing and hear the live region announcements. The relationship should be programmatically determinable per WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In characterCounter.attach(), after creating counterEl and inserting it, add: const counterId = srUtils.ensureId(counterEl, 'char-counter'); field.setAttribute('aria-describedby', ((field.getAttribute('aria-describedby') || '') + ' ' + counterId).trim());

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-hidden when dynamically hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer controlled by the `mobileNav` module is shown and hidden by toggling CSS classes (`is-open`, `nav-open`). When the drawer is closed via `mobileNav.close()`, the code removes these classes but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element. Screen reader users may still perceive the hidden drawer content in the accessibility tree even when it is visually hidden via CSS.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1684: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` inside the `close()` function after removing the open classes.
  Line 1654: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` inside the `open()` function when revealing the drawer, or remove the attribute entirely.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner does not hide background content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the cookie consent banner (modal dialog) is displayed, it uses `aria-modal="true"` which has varying support across browsers and assistive technologies. The code does not programmatically set `aria-hidden="true"` on the main page content siblings to ensure they are hidden from screen reader users while the modal is open, potentially allowing navigation outside the modal.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3194: After showing the banner and activating the focus trap, add code to set `aria-hidden="true"` on the main content container (e.g., `qs('main')` or `qs('#main-content')`) and restore it to `false` or remove it in the `_hideBanner()` function when the banner is dismissed.

issuesFound.title: Contact form full name field missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form includes an input field with `name="full_name"` that collects personal data (user's full name). The JavaScript initialization does not programmatically set the `autocomplete` attribute on this field. Without autocomplete, browsers cannot assist users with filling the field, failing WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2768: After selecting the field with `qs('[name="full_name"]', _formEl)`, add `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');` to enable browser autofill.

issuesFound.title: Contact form email field missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form includes an input field with `name="email"` that collects personal email addresses. The initialization code does not set the `autocomplete` attribute to "email", preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill and failing WCAG 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2768: After selecting the email field, add `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`.

issuesFound.title: Wizard form full name field missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard contains an input with `name="wizard_name"` in step 1 that collects the user's full name. The wizard initialization binds validation but does not set `autocomplete="name"` on this personal data field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2950: After selecting the field with `qs('[name="wizard_name"]', step.panelEl)`, add `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`.

issuesFound.title: Wizard form email field missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard contains an input with `name="wizard_email"` collecting personal email data. The initialization does not set `autocomplete="email"`, failing to identify the input purpose for assistive technologies and browsers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2950: After selecting the wizard email field, add `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`.

issuesFound.title: Wizard form cardholder name field missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Step 2 of the wizard includes `name="wizard_card_name"` for the cardholder's name. This is personal data (name on credit card) and should have `autocomplete="cc-name"` to allow browsers to autofill payment information securely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2950: After selecting the card name field, add `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'cc-name');`.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription form captures email addresses via `qs('input[type="email"]', _formEl)` but does not set `autocomplete="email"`. This prevents password managers and browsers from suggesting the user's email address.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2640: After selecting `_emailEl`, add `_emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu controller opens and closes dropdown panels when users click triggers or navigate with keyboard, but it does not write to an aria-live region to announce the expanded or collapsed state to screen reader users. While aria-expanded is toggled on the trigger, the content change is not explicitly announced, leaving blind users unaware that the menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3189: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 3230: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without user-controllable pause
issuesFound.explanation: The toastManager displays notifications that automatically dismiss after a configurable duration using setTimeout. There is no mechanism to pause, stop, or extend the timer on hover, focus, or user interaction. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 (Timing Adjustable) as users with reading disabilities or those using screen readers may not have sufficient time to consume the notification content before it disappears.

issuesFound.title: Character counter not programmatically associated with input field
issuesFound.explanation: The characterCounter module creates a live counter element displaying remaining characters and inserts it into the DOM after the input field. However, it does not associate the counter with the input using aria-describedby. Screen reader users therefore cannot discover the character limit information when focusing the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4140: After inserting counterEl, add `field.setAttribute('aria-describedby', counterEl.id);` to link the input to the counter.

issuesFound.title: File upload selected file list not associated with input
issuesFound.explanation: When files are selected in the fileUploadField, a list of selected files is rendered in the DOM. This list provides important status information (file names, sizes, upload progress) but is not programmatically associated with the file input element via aria-describedby. Screen reader users focusing the upload zone will not be informed of the currently selected files.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4670: After creating the listEl, add `inputEl.setAttribute('aria-describedby', listEl.id);` and ensure listEl has a unique id via `srUtils.ensureId(listEl, 'upload-list')`.

issuesFound.title: Wizard step panels incorrectly marked as tab panels without tab controls
issuesFound.explanation: The multiStepWizard assigns role="tabpanel" to each step panel container. However, there is no corresponding tablist structure, tab buttons with aria-controls, or aria-expanded attributes linking the navigation buttons to these panels. This creates a mismatch with the ARIA tab pattern and causes screen readers to announce the content incorrectly. The wizard should use role="region" or no explicit role instead, or implement proper tabs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4523: Remove `step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'tabpanel');` and instead use `step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'region');` or rely on the implicit section role.

issuesFound.title: Product grid not marked as busy during filter animation
issuesFound.explanation: When the user applies filters, the product grid undergoes a visible animation where cards are hidden and shown. During this time, the grid is visually updating but does not have aria-busy="true" set. Screen reader users may attempt to navigate the grid while it is in an unstable state, encountering disappearing or reappearing elements unexpectedly.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3745: Before calling `filterAnimation.run`, add `srUtils.setBusy(_gridEl, true);` 
  Line 3758: In the onDone callback of filterAnimation.run, add `srUtils.setBusy(_gridEl, false);`

issuesFound.title: Search input not marked as busy during suggestion fetch
issuesFound.explanation: While the search component is fetching suggestions from the data source, the input field remains in a loading state visually (implied by the async operation) but does not have aria-busy="true" set. Screen reader users are not informed that the search suggestions are being updated and that the results list is unstable.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2450: After setting `_searchState.loading = true;`, add `srUtils.setBusy(_inputEl, true);`
  Line 2460: After setting `_searchState.loading = false;`, add `srUtils.setBusy(_inputEl, false);`

issuesFound.title: File upload zone not marked as busy during upload
issuesFound.explanation: When files are being uploaded via XMLHttpRequest, the upload zone shows progress indicators but the zone element itself does not have aria-busy="true" set. Screen reader users interacting with the zone during upload are not informed that an operation is in progress and that the file list content is changing.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4725: At the start of _uploadEntry, add `srUtils.setBusy(binding.zoneEl, true);`
  Line 4745: In the load and error handlers, add `srUtils.setBusy(binding.zoneEl, false);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce that the menu is expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2032: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. The companion _closeInstance function sets it to "false", so the state becomes out of sync when the menu opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2143: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the open() function animates the panel and removes the hidden attribute but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", leaving the attribute in the wrong state for open items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3548: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _open function that displays the search suggestions list sets internal state but does not update aria-expanded on the combobox input element. The _close function sets it to "false", so screen readers report the list as collapsed even when it is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2732: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies cannot identify the field as being in an error state, failing WCAG 3.3.1 Error Identification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1232: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the null checks and before `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: Although the code declares a _trap variable and references it in close(), the open() function never instantiates createFocusTrap. Keyboard users can Tab out of the open mobile menu into the background page, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2038 (after _lockScroll): Insert the following two lines:
  `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger, escapeDeactivates: true });`
  `_trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle the dropdown visibility but do not announce the state change to screen readers. Users navigating with keyboard or screen readers receive no feedback that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2146 (after emit in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`
  Line 2159 (after emit in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function toggles the mobile navigation drawer visible but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert after `_open = true;` in mobileNav.open function: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the function fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state of the disclosure widget.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert after `inst.open = true;` in _openInstance function: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion open function adds the open CSS class and reveals the panel but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive that the answer is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert after `item.open = true;` in faqAccordion.open function: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid helper links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` in markInvalid function: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown panels changes the visible content but does not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert at end of _openInstance function: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` Line: Insert at end of _closeInstance function (before focus return): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The _trap variable is declared but never assigned a createFocusTrap instance. When the mobile menu opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing keyboard users to tab outside into the background page.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.init, after `_initialised = true;`, add: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true });` Line: In mobileNav.open, replace the check with: `if (_trap) _trap.activate();` Line: In mobileNav.close, replace the check with: `if (_trap) _trap.deactivate();`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close via Escape loses focus
issuesFound.explanation: When the Escape key closes an FAQ item, the close function does not return focus to the trigger button. Keyboard users are sent to the top of the document or to an unexpected location.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In _onKeydown ESCAPE case, change `close(inst);` to `close(inst, false, true);` Line: Update close function signature to `function close(item, announceToSR, returnFocus) {` Line: Insert before closing brace of close function: `if (returnFocus) item.trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is shown and hidden via CSS classes, but aria-hidden is not toggled. When closed, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree if CSS only moves it off-screen rather than using display:none.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.open, after opening classes added: `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');` Line: In mobileNav.close, before removing classes: `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true"

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"

issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the FAQ answer panel but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". This is essential for screen readers to convey the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3005: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` at the start of the open function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function collapses the FAQ answer panel but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 3035: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the start of the close function (replacing the existing commented line or adding if missing).

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing aria-pressed on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function toggles between monthly and annual billing but does not update the aria-pressed attribute on the toggle button to indicate which state is active. This is required for toggle buttons to communicate their pressed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2520: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, period === 'year');` inside the setPeriod function after updating the period state.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play function missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.play function starts auto-rotation but does not set aria-pressed="true" on the play/pause button to indicate that the "play" state is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3248: Add `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true');` inside the play function.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel pause function missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.pause function stops auto-rotation but does not set aria-pressed="false" on the play/pause button to indicate that the "pause" state is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3258: Add `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'false');` inside the pause function.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher set function missing aria-pressed on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher.set function updates the visual theme but does not update the aria-pressed attribute on the theme toggle buttons to indicate which theme is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3850: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, btnTheme === pref ? 'true' : 'false');` inside the forEach loop that updates toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. This attribute is required to communicate the invalid state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2092: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid missing aria-invalid clear
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes error styling and descriptions but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (or set it to "false"), potentially leaving stale invalid state information on the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2105: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` inside the markValid function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function dynamically updates the visible product count and grid layout but does not announce these changes via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2420: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products shown`);` after calculating totalMatches in the applyFilters function.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement for price changes
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates price amounts visually but does not announce the new prices to screen readers via a live region, leaving users unaware of the price change when toggling billing periods.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2525: Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);` inside the setPeriod function.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop _onClick missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce this navigation action via a live region. Users may be disoriented when focus moves without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1810: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');` at the start of the _onClick function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing focus return to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function deactivates the focus trap but does not return keyboard focus to the trigger button that opened the drawer. Keyboard users expect focus to return to the triggering element when a modal/drawer closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1542: Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` at the end of the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function does not move keyboard focus into the navigation drawer when it opens. Focus should be moved to the first focusable element inside the drawer or the drawer itself to ensure keyboard users can navigate the new content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1518: Add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` after opening the drawer.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel using CSS classes but does not immediately set aria-hidden="true" on the panel. While the hidden attribute is set after a timeout, the delay leaves the panel in the accessibility tree while visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1725: Add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` at the start of the _closeInstance function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function begins animating the panel closed but does not immediately set aria-hidden="true". The panel remains in the accessibility tree until the animation completes and the hidden attribute is set.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3035: Add `setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` at the start of the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element, leaving it accessible to screen readers even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1542: Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` inside the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing aria-hidden clear
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function reveals the drawer but does not set aria-hidden="false" (or remove aria-hidden) to ensure the drawer is exposed to the accessibility tree when visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1518: Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');` or `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);` after revealing the drawer.

faqAccordion close function missing aria-expanded update
