issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility by adding/removing CSS classes and managing the _open state variable, but they do not call setAttribute('aria-expanded', ...) on the _trigger element to reflect the open/closed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2065: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); inside the open function after setting _open = true. Line 2091: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); inside the close function after setting _open = false.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function only links the error message element via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to convey validation state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1427: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation state, the function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not set aria-invalid="false" (or remove the attribute), leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: Add removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID); after the if-else block that updates aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce dynamic content changes
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products and updating the visible count and empty state, the function emits a custom event but does not write to an aria-live region to announce the result count (e.g., "5 products found") to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2539: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`); after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches);

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When switching billing periods (monthly/annual), prices update visually with animation, but no live region announcement informs screen reader users of the price change or the new billing period selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2849: Add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`); after the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The initialization function finds the trigger and drawer elements but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button to indicate the initial closed state of the mobile navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2148: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after _initialised = true;

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons use aria-selected without ensuring role="tab"
issuesFound.explanation: The function sets aria-selected on period buttons, which is only valid for elements with role="tab" inside a role="tablist" container. The code does not ensure these roles are present on the elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2886: Add if (!btn.hasAttribute('role')) setAttr(btn, ARIA.ROLE, 'tab'); inside the forEach. Also ensure the container has role="tablist" in the HTML or add it via JS in pricing.init.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard._updateProgress sets ARIA attributes without role="progressbar"
issuesFound.explanation: The function sets aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext on the progress bar element, but does not ensure the element has role="progressbar". Without the role, assistive technologies may not recognize it as a progress indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3282: Add if (!_progressBar.getAttribute('role')) setAttr(_progressBar, ARIA.ROLE, 'progressbar'); before setting aria-valuenow.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav references uninitialized focus trap variable
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions reference _trap (a focus trap instance), but the variable is declared as null and never initialized within the shown code, meaning focus trapping is not actually activated when the menu opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2130-2153 (init function): Initialize _trap by calling createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }) and store the returned controller in _trap. Then call _trap.activate() in the open function and _trap.deactivate() in the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeAll does not return focus when closing via outside click
issuesFound.explanation: When clicking outside the navigation to close dropdowns, _closeAll is called without the returnFocus parameter, so focus is not returned to the trigger button, potentially leaving focus in an unexpected location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2193: Change _closeAll(); to _closeAll(null, true); or modify _closeAll to accept a returnFocus parameter and pass it to _closeInstance. Alternatively, track the currently open instance and return focus to its trigger when closing via outside click.

fpTitles: pricing period buttons use aria-selected without ensuring role="tab"
multiStepWizard._updateProgress sets ARIA attributes without role="progressbar"
dropdownMenu._closeAll does not return focus when closing via outside click

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in dropdownMenu manage the visibility of dropdown panels but fail to set aria-expanded="true" or "false" on the trigger buttons. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2000: Inside _openInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `inst.open = true;`.
  Line 2020: Inside _closeInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The site search input acts as a combobox that shows/hides a suggestion listbox, but the _open() and _close() functions do not toggle aria-expanded. Screen readers cannot detect when the suggestion list is displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2700: Inside _open(), add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`.
  Line 2710: Inside _close(), add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) in the pricing module use CSS classes to indicate active state but do not expose this state to assistive technologies via aria-pressed.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function links error messages to fields via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot identify fields with validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1600: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` as the last line inside the markInvalid() function, after the addDescribedBy() call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes error descriptions but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. Fields remain marked as invalid to assistive technologies after being corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1610: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` at the end of the markValid() function, after the if block that updates aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). Consequently, keyboard focus is not trapped within the mobile navigation drawer when it opens, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function accepts an announceToSR parameter but never uses it. When the navigation opens, no announcement is made to screen readers, leaving users unaware that content has appeared.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() function accepts an announceToSR parameter but never utilizes it. Expanding an FAQ item is not announced to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods (monthly/annual), prices update visually but the change is not announced via the live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the price update.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results
issuesFound.explanation: After applying filters, the product grid updates and result counts change, but no announcement is made to screen readers regarding the number of results or the active filter.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, they are not initialized with aria-expanded="false". Assistive technologies cannot detect that the menus are initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2100: Inside register(), after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add:
  ```
  setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);
  ```

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input combobox is initialized without aria-expanded="false", so screen readers do not know the suggestion list is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2800: Inside init(), in the section that wires ARIA attributes, add:
  ```
  setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
  ```
  after the line `setAttr(input, 'aria-controls', _listId);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes (via Escape key or close button), focus is not returned to the hamburger trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1870: Inside close(), after `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate();`, add:
  ```
  if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();
  ```

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle element (when not a native button) is given role="switch" but aria-checked is never updated to reflect the current state (month vs annual).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3400: Inside _onToggleClick(), add after `const next = _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'month' : 'year';`:
  ```
  if (_toggleEl) setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', next === 'year');
  ```
  Also in init(), after setting the role, add initial state:
  ```
  setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', _pricingState.period === 'year');
  ```

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions omit aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions manage dropdown panel visibility but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3960 (in _openInstance, after `inst.open = true;`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 3990 (in _closeInstance, after `inst.open = false;`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions omit aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions animate FAQ panels and update internal state but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger buttons, leaving assistive technology users unaware of the current expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5820 (in open function, after `item.open = true;`): `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 5870 (in close function, after `item.open = false;`): `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid helper function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers do not announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3285 (in markInvalid function, after addDescribedBy call): `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons lack aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) function as a two-state toggle but do not update aria-pressed to indicate the active state to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid omits aria-invalid cleanup
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid helper removes error descriptions but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (set to "false" or remove it), potentially leaving fields marked as invalid when they are actually valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3305 (in markValid function, end of function): `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" during init(), leaving screen readers unaware of its expandable nature on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3730 (in init function, after _trigger assignment): `if (_trigger && !_trigger.getAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are registered without initialising aria-expanded="false", violating the requirement that expandable widgets indicate their initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4080 (in register function, after setting aria-controls): `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input with role="combobox" is not initialised with aria-expanded="false", causing screen readers to not recognize it as a collapsed popup on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4380 (in suggestionListRenderer.mount, after setting aria-haspopup): `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _open() and _close() functions in siteSearch toggle the suggestion list visibility but do not update aria-expanded on the combobox input to reflect the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4450 (in _open function): `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 4480 (in _close function): `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it remains in the accessibility tree because it is only hidden via CSS classes, not via the hidden attribute or aria-hidden. When open, aria-hidden is not removed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3565 (in open function, after adding classes): `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`
  Line 3635 (in close function, after removing classes): `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter result updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the result count updates visually but no aria-live announcement informs screen reader users of the change in product count or the active filter category.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4745 (in applyFilters, inside filterAnimation.run callback): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates price displays visually but does not announce the change or the new prices to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5090 (in setPeriod function, after emit): `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the page context significantly but is not announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3568 (in open function, before emit): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Line 3638 (in close function, before emit): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced, leaving screen reader users unaware that content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3968 (in _openInstance, after setting aria-expanded): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`
  Line 4003 (in _closeInstance, after setting aria-expanded): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips removal not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Removing a filter chip updates the UI but does not announce the removal or the updated filter state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4970 (in _onChipClick, after productFilter method calls): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterRemoved || 'Filter removed');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing individual FAQ items is not announced via a live region, so screen reader users do not receive feedback when activating a question.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5825 (in open function, after setting aria-expanded): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : 'Answer expanded');`
  Line 5880 (in close function, after setting aria-expanded): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : 'Answer collapsed');`

issuesFound.title: contactForm inputs lack autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields (full name, email, subject, message) do not have autocomplete attributes, preventing browsers from filling them automatically and assistive technologies from identifying their purpose per WCAG 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 6720 (after binding name field): `if (!_field.getAttribute('autocomplete')) setAttr(_field, 'autocomplete', 'name');` (for full_name field)
  Line 6725 (after binding email field): `if (!_field.getAttribute('autocomplete')) setAttr(_field, 'autocomplete', 'email');` (for email field)

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email lacks autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input does not have autocomplete="email", preventing automatic filling and proper identification by assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6625 (in init, after _emailEl assignment): `if (_emailEl && !_emailEl.getAttribute('autocomplete')) setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop activation not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Activating the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page but does not announce the action completion to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3880 (in _onClick function): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded updates

  Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance(): After `inst.open = false;` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to true (though it is correctly set to false when closing). Screen readers cannot convey the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open(): After `item.open = true;` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Search combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but fails to update aria-expanded when the suggestion list appears or disappears. Screen reader users cannot tell when suggestions are available or hidden.

Line siteSearch._open(): After `_searchState.open = true;` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line siteSearch._close(): After `if (!_searchState.open) return;` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons do not set aria-pressed to reflect the active filter state, preventing screen reader users from knowing which category filter is currently applied.

issuesFound.title: Form validation not setting aria-invalid

Line srUtils.markInvalid(): After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`
  Line srUtils.markValid(): After the aria-describedby cleanup, add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger not initialized with aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers not initialized with aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are registered without an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute, failing to communicate their initial collapsed state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Search input not initialized with aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is not initialized with aria-expanded="false". Screen readers may assume the suggestion list is visible by default, causing confusion about the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch.init(): After `suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer is not announced to screen reader users via a live region, leaving them unaware that the navigation context has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.open(): Before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', ...)` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Line mobileNav.close(): Before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...)` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu open and close actions are not announced, preventing screen reader users from knowing when menu content appears or disappears.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing periods updates prices visually but provides no audible confirmation to screen reader users about the change or the new pricing.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod(): After `_syncToggleState(period);` add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count and grid changes are not announced, leaving screen reader users unaware that the content has updated or how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters(): Inside the `filterAnimation.run` callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) || 'Showing ${s.activeCategory} products');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is not marked with aria-hidden="true", so screen reader users may still be able to navigate to off-screen drawer content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.close(): After `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`
  Line mobileNav.open(): After `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` or `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing users to tab into background content. Additionally, focus is not returned to the trigger button when the drawer closes.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation trigger not initialized with aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: Modal backdrop has incorrect aria-modal attribute placement
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal backdrop overlay instead of the dialog element itself. This causes assistive technologies to incorrectly identify the modal boundary and may fail to restrict navigation to the modal content, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field error message not connected
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives an "error" prop but the interface expects "errorMessage", and the "errorId" prop is missing. Consequently, the error message element is never rendered, breaking the aria-describedby reference on the input and preventing error announcement to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line N: <FormGroup
  Line N:   label="Email address"
  Line N:   htmlFor="newsletter-email"
  Line N:   errorId="newsletter-email-error"
  Line N:   errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
  Line N: >

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay incorrectly has aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The modal-overlay div has aria-modal="true", but this attribute should be on the dialog element itself. Having it on the overlay may cause assistive technologies to incorrectly handle the modal context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1872: Remove aria-modal="true" from the modal-overlay div.

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The dialog div has role="dialog" but lacks aria-modal="true", which is necessary to indicate that the dialog is modal and that the rest of the page is inert while the dialog is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1873-1880: Add aria-modal="true" to the modal-dialog div props.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div onClick without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has an onClick handler to close the dialog but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers for Enter and Space. Keyboard-only users cannot activate the backdrop click to close.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2530: Replace the div with a button element styled as an overlay, or add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler: `<div className="modal-overlay" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-label="Close dialog" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute misplaced

issuesFound.fix: Line ~2530: Remove aria-modal="true" from the overlay div. Line ~2532: Add aria-modal="true" to the dialog div: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>`
